text: The surgeon performs a mini laparotomy in the midline of the abdomen, preparing for the NM block exteriorization of the specimen. The incision may be lengthened due to the large size of the inflammatory tumor involving multiple bowel segments.

The surgeon performs small bowel semiology, examining the proximal intestine for complications, either laparoscopically or through a mini-laparotomy. The thickened intestinal mesentery is ligated extracorporeally using reabsorbable stitches to minimize the risk of postoperative bleeding.
